functions.name: addLogto
functions.description: Adds a Logto resource to the Aspire distributed application by configuring it with the specified name, associated PostgreSQL server resource, and database name.
functions.returns: The resource builder configured for the added Logto resource.
functions.signature: addLogto(name: string, postgres: PostgresServerResource, databaseName?: string, port?: number, adminPort?: number): LogtoResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the Logto resource.

parameters.name: postgres
parameters.description: The resource builder for the PostgreSQL server that the Logto will connect to.

parameters.name: databaseName
parameters.description: The PostgreSQL database name Logto should use.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: The host port to be configured for the primary endpoint. If `null`, Aspire will assign a random host port.

parameters.name: adminPort
parameters.description: The host port to be configured for the administrative endpoint. If `null`, Aspire will assign a random host port.

functions.name: uriExpression
functions.description: Gets the connection URI expression for the Logto container resource.

functions.name: withAdminEndpoint
functions.description: Configures the specified Logto resource to include an administrative endpoint with the given URL.
functions.returns: The resource builder for the configured Logto resource.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL of the administrative endpoint to be used for the Logto resource.

functions.name: withDatabase
functions.description: Configures the Logto resource to connect to the specified PostgreSQL database.
functions.returns: The resource builder for the configured Logto resource.

parameters.name: postgres
parameters.description: The resource builder for the PostgreSQL server to connect to.

parameters.name: databaseName
parameters.description: The PostgreSQL database name Logto should use.

functions.name: withDatabaseSeeding
functions.description: Starts Logto by running the database seed command before the application process.
functions.returns: The resource builder for the configured Logto resource.

functions.name: withDataBindMount
functions.description: Configures the Logto resource to use a data bind mount with the specified source directory.
functions.returns: The resource builder for the configured Logto resource.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The host directory to be mounted as the data volume.

functions.name: withDataVolume
functions.description: Configures the Logto resource with a data volume, allowing persistent storage.
functions.returns: The resource builder configured with the specified data volume.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The optional name of the data volume. If not provided, a default name is generated.

functions.name: withDeprecationTracing
functions.description: Enables Node.js deprecation tracing for the Logto by setting the NODE_OPTIONS environment variable to '--trace-deprecation'. This allows stack traces to be printed for deprecated API usage.
functions.returns: The resource builder for the configured Logto resource.

functions.name: withDisableAdminConsole
functions.description: Configures the Logto resource to disable the Admin Console port.
functions.returns: The resource builder for the configured Logto resource.

parameters.name: disable
parameters.description: A value indicating whether to disable the Admin Console port.

functions.name: withNodeEnv
functions.description: Configures the Logto resource to use the specified Node.js environment value by setting the corresponding environment variable.
functions.returns: The resource builder for the configured Logto resource.

parameters.name: env
parameters.description: The value of the Node.js environment variable to set, typically "development", "production", or "test".

functions.name: withRedis
functions.description: Configures the Logto resource to use a specified Redis resource for caching or other functionality.
functions.returns: The resource builder configured with the specified Redis resource.

parameters.name: redis
parameters.description: The resource builder for the Redis resource to be used by the Logto resource.

functions.name: withResourcePort
functions.description: Configures HTTP endpoints for the given Logto resource builder with specified port settings.
functions.returns: The updated resource builder with the configured HTTP endpoints.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: The host port to be configured for the primary endpoint. If `null`, Aspire will assign a random host port.

parameters.name: adminPort
parameters.description: The host port to be configured for the administrative endpoint. If `null`, Aspire will assign a random host port.

functions.name: withSecretVault
functions.description: Configures the Logto resource to use a secret vault with the specified key encryption key (KEK).
functions.returns: The resource builder for the configured Logto resource.

parameters.name: secretVaultKek
parameters.description: The base64-encoded key encryption key (KEK) for the secret vault.

functions.name: withSensitiveUsername
functions.description: Specifies whether the username is case-sensitive.
functions.returns: The updated resource builder with the configured case-sensitivity setting.

parameters.name: sensitiveUsername
parameters.description: A value indicating whether usernames should be treated as case-sensitive.

functions.name: withTrustProxyHeader
functions.description: Configures the Logto resource to enable or disable the trust proxy header behavior.
functions.returns: The resource builder for the configured Logto resource.

parameters.name: trustProxyHeader
parameters.description: A value indicating whether to trust the proxy header.

handleTypes.name: LogtoResource
handleTypes.description: Represents a containerized resource specific to Logto that extends the base functionality of container resources by providing additional endpoint and connection string management.
handleTypes.remarks:
This class is designed for use in an application hosting environment and incorporates
a primary HTTP endpoint with predefined default port configurations.
